summary: Tested assistant message UI polish changes (ChatGPT/Emergent style). All 12 test cases PASSED. The UI changes are working correctly: assistant messages have no heavy card background, action buttons appear on hover, no intent badges shown, smaller circular avatars, and cleaner typography.

features_tested.feature: Open 'Build a hello world page' project with conversation
Assistant messages render without heavy card background
features_tested.details: Assistant message wrapper has 'rounded-xl px-1 py-0.5' without bg/border

features_tested.feature: User messages have subtle cyan background card
No intent badges shown in chat messages
PlanCard renders for messages with proposedPlan
features_tested.details: 'Ready to build' PlanCard visible with Build it/Change something/Cancel buttons

features_tested.feature: Message text readable with proper typography

assistant_message_styling.description: Assistant messages now have minimal styling without heavy card background
assistant_message_styling.old_classes: bg-[rgba(20,20,56,0.5)] border border-[rgba(124,58,237,0.1)] rounded-xl px-3.5 py-2.5

user_message_styling.description: User messages retain subtle cyan background

action_buttons_hover.description: Action buttons use opacity-0 group-hover/msg:opacity-100 for hover reveal

intent_badges_removed.description: Intent badges line removed entirely from normal chat messages

typography.description: em-prose class with 13.5px font and tighter margins

seed_data_creation: None - used existing 'Build a hello world page' project
context_for_next_testing_agent: Assistant message UI polish changes fully tested and working. Key findings: (1) Assistant messages have no heavy card background - just 'rounded-xl px-1 py-0.5' without bg/border. (2) User messages retain subtle cyan background 'rgba(0, 229, 255, 0.06)'. (3) Action buttons (copy, regen, thumbs) use 'opacity-0 group-hover/msg:opacity-100' for hover reveal - verified initial opacity 0, hover opacity 1. (4) Avatars are smaller (20px) and circular (rounded-full). (5) No intent badges shown. (6) PlanCard still renders for proposed plans. (7) Typography uses em-prose class with 13.5px font.
